content.full_text:
P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 ••• 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814 
Technical Assistanc8' 
(916) 322·5662 
Mike Kelly, Director 
Administration 
322·5660 
Executive/le<,;al 
322-5901 
March 4, 1983 
Enforcement 
322-64.41 
Statements of Economic Interest 
322-6444 
Shafter Recreation and Park District 
505 Sunset 
Shafter, CA 
93263 
Dear Mr. Kelly: 
Re: 
Your Request for Advice, Our 
Advice No. A-83-05l 
Thank you for your request for advice on behalf of David 
Mizener, a member of the Shafter Parks and Recreation District 
Board. 
The Recreation District is considering entering into a 
joint powers agreement with the Kern High School District for 
the construction of softball fields. 
Your question is whether 
Mr. Mizener, as a teacher for the High School District, may 
participate in the discussions and decisions related to this 
issue. 
The Political Reform Act prevents a public official from 
making, or participating in the making of a governmental 
decision in which he knows or has reason to know he has a 
financial interest. 
(Government Code Section 87100.1:,/) 
An 
official has a "financial interest" in a decision within the 
meaning of Section 87100 if it is reasonably foreseeable that 
the decision will materially effect a source of income 
aggregating $250 or more received by the public official within 
12 months prior to the time when the decision is made. 
Section 82030 (b) (2) excludes from the defini tion of "income" 
salary received from a local government agency. 
Thus, the High 
School District is not a source of income to Mr. Mizener within 
the meaning of the Act and he is not pronibited from making and 
1/ All statutory references made are to the Government 
Code unless otherwise indicated. 


Mike Kelly, Director 
March 4, 1983 
Page Two 
participating in the making of the decisions related to the 
joint agreement or the proposed construction. 
I hope I have clarified this matter for you. 
If you have 
any additional questions, please feel free to contact me at 
916/322-5901. 
~ Very. truly y,ours, 
)r£,~~~~ 
C I~CL-v 
. 
f 
........ JJanis Shank McLean 
Counsel 
Legal Division 
JSM:km

sections.question: Your question is whether Mr. Mizener, as a teacher for the High School District, may participate in the discussions and decisions related to this issue.
sections.conclusion: Thus, the High School District is not a source of income to Mr. Mizener within the meaning of the Act and he is not prohibited from making and participating in the making of the decisions related to the joint agreement or the proposed construction.
sections.question_synthetic: Whether a Parks and Recreation District board member who is employed as a teacher by the High School District may participate in board discussions and decisions regarding a proposed joint powers agreement between the two districts for softball field construction.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The board member may participate in the discussions and decisions because his salary from the High School District is excluded from the definition of 'income' under Government Code Section 82030(b)(2), meaning he has no disqualifying financial interest in the decision.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Well-structured advice letter with clear question and conclusion sections. Document number A-83-051 dated March 4, 1983. Sent to Mike Kelly regarding David Mizener.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Your question is whether Mr. Mizener, as a teacher for the High School District, may participate in the discussions and decisions related to this issue.

CONCLUSION: Thus, the High School District is not a source of income to Mr. Mizener within the meaning of the Act and he is not prohibited from making and participating in the making of the decisions related to the joint agreement or the proposed construction.

embedding.summary: FPPC advised that a Shafter Parks and Recreation District board member who is employed as a teacher by Kern High School District is not prohibited from participating in discussions regarding a joint powers agreement between the two districts, as salary from a local government agency is excluded from the definition of 'financial interest' under the Political Reform Act.
